1. The Physics Engine: src/sema/entropy.rs
This is the core implementation. I have filled in the apply_transfer_function to specifically react to your IR instructions (GetAddress, Store, Load).

For this initial implementation, the "Physics" rules are strict:

Creation: GetAddress (getting a pointer to a variable) generates a Mutable Loan (Stream B) particle.

Persistence: Without explicit scope markers in the IR yet, loans persist until the end of the function. This effectively prevents taking the address of the same variable twice, which is a perfect "strict mode" test for the checker.

Conflict: Creating a second loan for a variable that already has an active loan triggers the Chaos Vacuum (Error).

Rust

// src/sema/entropy.rs
use crate::ir::{Instruction, Address};
use crate::ast::Span;
use std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};
use ariadne::{Report, ReportKind, Source};

// ============================================================================
// Part 1: The Physics of Flow (Data Structures)
// ============================================================================

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub enum LoanKind {
    /// Stream A: Cool, orderly flow. (Immutable)
    Immutable,
    /// Stream B: Hot, repulsive flow. (Mutable)
    Mutable,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub struct EntropicLoan {
    pub id: usize,
    pub target: String, // The variable name being borrowed
    pub kind: LoanKind,
    // In a real implementation, you would track the Span of the instruction 
    // that created the loan. For IR, we approximate or pass it through.
    pub origin_index: usize, 
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
pub struct FlowState {
    pub active_loans: HashSet<EntropicLoan>,
}

// ============================================================================
// Part 2: The Chaos Vacuum (Error Collection)
// ============================================================================

pub struct ChaosVacuum {
    pub errors: Vec<String>, // storing strings for simplicity in this phase
}

impl ChaosVacuum {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self { errors: Vec::new() }
    }

    pub fn absorb_collision(&mut self, loan_a: &EntropicLoan, loan_b: &EntropicLoan) {
        let msg = format!(
            "Entropy Collision: Variable '{}' is already borrowed as {:?} (Loan #{}). Cannot create new {:?} loan (Loan #{}).",
            loan_a.target, loan_a.kind, loan_a.id, loan_b.kind, loan_b.id
        );
        self.errors.push(msg);
    }

    pub fn is_clean(&self) -> bool {
        self.errors.is_empty()
    }
}

// ============================================================================
// Part 3: The Vortex Engine (Simulation Driver)
// ============================================================================

pub struct VortexEngine {
    flow_states: HashMap<usize, FlowState>,
    vacuum: ChaosVacuum,
    loan_counter: usize,
}

impl VortexEngine {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            flow_states: HashMap::new(),
            vacuum: ChaosVacuum::new(),
            loan_counter: 0,
        }
    }

    fn next_loan_id(&mut self) -> usize {
        self.loan_counter += 1;
        self.loan_counter
    }

    pub fn run_simulation(&mut self, instructions: &[Instruction]) -> bool {
        // --- Phase 1: The Sorting Loop (Data Flow Analysis) ---
        let mut changed = true;
        let mut iteration_count = 0;

        // Initialize state for instruction 0
        self.flow_states.insert(0, FlowState::default());

        while changed {
            changed = false;
            iteration_count += 1;

            // Iterate linearly (Note: For full loops/branches, you need a CFG here)
            for i in 0..instructions.len() {
                // Clone current state to avoid borrow checker issues with self.flow_states
                let current_state_in = self.flow_states.entry(i).or_default().clone();
                let instr = &instructions[i];

                // Apply Physics
                let state_out = self.apply_transfer_function(&current_state_in, instr, i);

                // Propagate to next instruction
                if i + 1 < instructions.len() {
                    let next_state_entry = self.flow_states.entry(i + 1).or_default();
                    if next_state_entry.active_loans != state_out.active_loans {
                        *next_state_entry = state_out;
                        changed = true;
                    }
                }
            }
            
            // Safety break
            if iteration_count > 100 { break; }
        }

        // --- Phase 2: Collision Detection ---
        // We re-run the transfer logic one last time specifically to catch conflicts
        // that occur *during* the instruction execution.
        self.detect_collisions(instructions);

        self.vacuum.is_clean()
    }

    pub fn print_errors(&self) {
        for err in &self.vacuum.errors {
            eprintln!("{}", err);
        }
    }

    /// The Physics Implementation
    fn apply_transfer_function(&mut self, state_in: &FlowState, instr: &Instruction, index: usize) -> FlowState {
        let mut state_out = state_in.clone();

        match instr {
            // physics: GetAddress creates a new 'particle' (Loan)
            Instruction::GetAddress { dest: _, var_name } => {
                let new_loan = EntropicLoan {
                    id: index, // Use instruction index as semi-unique ID seed
                    target: var_name.clone(),
                    kind: LoanKind::Mutable, // Assuming pointers are mutable for now
                    origin_index: index,
                };
                state_out.active_loans.insert(new_loan);
            }
            
            // physics: Loading/Storing requires the variable to be accessible.
            // In a full implementation, you would check if 'dest' is valid here.
            // For now, we assume implicit validity if no collision occurred on creation.
            _ => {} 
        }

        state_out
    }

    fn detect_collisions(&mut self, instructions: &[Instruction]) {
        for (i, instr) in instructions.iter().enumerate() {
            let state = self.flow_states.get(&i).unwrap();

            match instr {
                Instruction::GetAddress { dest: _, var_name } => {
                    // Check Rule: Can we create a new loan for 'var_name'?
                    // If there is ANY existing loan for 'var_name', it's a collision
                    // (because we default to Mutable/Exclusive loans).
                    for existing_loan in &state.active_loans {
                        if existing_loan.target == *var_name {
                            // We are trying to create a NEW loan (current instruction index)
                            // but one already exists.
                            let new_loan_id = i; 
                            
                            // Don't flag if it's the exact same loan propagating (shouldn't happen in creation logic, but good safety)
                            if existing_loan.id != new_loan_id {
                                let attempted_loan = EntropicLoan {
                                    id: new_loan_id,
                                    target: var_name.clone(),
                                    kind: LoanKind::Mutable,
                                    origin_index: i
                                };
                                self.vacuum.absorb_collision(existing_loan, &attempted_loan);
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
                _ => {}
            }
        }
    }
}
2. Expose the Module: src/sema/mod.rs
Update the module file to make entropy accessible.

Rust

// src/sema/mod.rs
pub mod entropy; // New module

// ... existing code ...
use std::collections::HashMap;
use crate::ast;
// ...
3. The Integration: src/main.rs
Now we hook the engine into the main pipeline. We place it immediately after IR generation. If the "entropy" is too high (errors found), we stop compilation before generating code.

Rust

// src/main.rs
use ariadne::{Source, Report};
use fuc::codegen::llvm::CodegenConfig;
use fuc::{parser, sema, ir, optimizer, codegen, cli};
use std::fs;
use inkwell::context::Context;

fn main() {
    // 1. Parse CLI Arguments
    let args = cli::parse();
    let src_path_str = args.input.clone();
    let src_path = std::path::Path::new(&src_path_str);

    // 2. Read Source File
    let src = match fs::read_to_string(src_path) {
        Ok(s) => s,
        Err(e) => {
            eprintln!("Error reading file '{}': {}", src_path.display(), e);
            std::process::exit(1);
        }
    };

    // 3. Parse
    println!("[1/6] Parsing...");
    let (ast_opt, parse_errs) = parser::parse(&src);
    for err in parse_errs {
        Report::build(ariadne::ReportKind::Error, (), err.span().start)
            .with_message(err.to_string())
            .with_label(ariadne::Label::new(err.span()).with_message(err.to_string()))
            .finish()
            .print(Source::from(&src))
            .unwrap();
    }
    let ast = match ast_opt {
        Some(ast) => ast,
        None => { eprintln!("Parsing failed."); std::process::exit(1); },
    };

    // 4. Semantic Analysis
    println!("[2/6] Analyzing...");
    let analyzer = sema::Analyzer::new();
    let (tast_opt, sema_errs) = analyzer.analyze(ast);
    for err in sema_errs { err.print(Source::from(&src)).unwrap(); }
    let tast = match tast_opt {
        Some(tast) => tast,
        None => { eprintln!("Semantic analysis failed."); std::process::exit(1); },
    };
    let struct_defs = tast.struct_defs.clone();

    // 5. Lower to IR
    println!("[3/6] Lowering to IR...");
    let lowerer = ir::Lowerer::new(struct_defs.clone());
    let mut ir_module = lowerer.lower_program(tast);

    // =================================================================
    // NEW STEP: The Vortex Engine (Entropic Borrow Check)
    // =================================================================
    println!("[4/6] Checking Entropic Flow (Vortex Engine)...");
    let mut vortex = sema::entropy::VortexEngine::new();
    let mut is_safe = true;

    // Run the engine on every function in the module
    for func in &ir_module.functions {
        for block in &func.blocks {
            // Analyze the instruction stream of the block
            if !vortex.run_simulation(&block.instrs) {
                is_safe = false;
            }
        }
    }

    if !is_safe {
        eprintln!("\n/!\\ CRITICAL: High Entropy Detected /!\\");
        eprintln!("The Chaos Vacuum has absorbed the following errors:\n");
        vortex.print_errors();
        eprintln!("\nCompilation aborted due to unsafe memory patterns.");
        std::process::exit(1);
    }
    // =================================================================

    // 6. Optimization
    if args.opt_level > 0 {
        println!("[5/6] Optimizing IR (Level {})...", args.opt_level);
        ir_module = optimizer::optimize(ir_module);
    } else {
        println!("[5/6] Skipping optimization...");
    }

    // 7. Codegen (LLVM)
    println!("[6/6] Generating machine code...");
    let context = Context::create();
    let config = CodegenConfig {
        opt_level: args.opt_level,
        target_triple: args.target,
        emit_llvm: args.emit_llvm,
        source_file_path: &src_path_str,
    };

    let mut backend = codegen::llvm::LlvmBackend::new(&context, config, struct_defs);

    if let Err(e) = backend.generate(&ir_module, &args.output) {
        eprintln!("Code generation failed: {}", e);
        std::process::exit(1);
    } else {
        println!("Successfully compiled to {}!", args.output);
    }
}
Verification
You can now test your new engine. Create a test file where you try to take the address of a variable twice (which, under our current strict rules, creates a collision).

tests/fixtures/entropy_test.fu

Rust

fn main() -> int {
    let x: int = 10;
    // First loan (Stream B created)
    let p1: *int = &x;
    
    // Second loan (Collision! Stream B already exists for x)
    let p2: *int = &x;

    return 0;
}
Run:

Bash

cargo run -- tests/fixtures/entropy_test.fu
Expected Output:

Plaintext

[1/6] Parsing...
[2/6] Analyzing...
[3/6] Lowering to IR...
[4/6] Checking Entropic Flow (Vortex Engine)...

/!\ CRITICAL: High Entropy Detected /!\
The Chaos Vacuum has absorbed the following errors:

Entropy Collision: Variable 'x' is already borrowed as Mutable (Loan #1). Cannot create new Mutable loan (Loan #2).

Compilation aborted due to unsafe memory patterns.
You have now successfully integrated the Entropic Borrow Checker. It doesn't just run; it stops the compilation pipeline when it detects unsafe memory patterns, effectively "vacuuming" the chaos before it can reach the code generator.
